new_classes.label_en: Other fruit spirit quality (excl. plum, pear, cherry)
new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a spirituous beverage obtained exclusively by alcoholic fermentation and distillation of fruit other than plums, pears or cherries (e.g. apricots, bilberries, raspberries, blackberries, blackcurrants, whitecurrants, redcurrants, strawberries, apples).

new_classes.label_en: Distillation process from fermented fruit mash (excl. plum, pear, cherry)
new_classes.label_de: Destillationsprozess aus vergorenem Obstbrei (ausgenommen Pflaume, Birne, Kirsche)
new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous distillation process in which the starting material is fermented mash of fruit other than plums, pears or cherries, such as apricots, bilberries, raspberries, blackberries, currants, strawberries or apples, yielding a fruit spirit classified under CN subheading 2208 90 48.

new_classes.label_en: Other fruit spirit (excl. plum, pear, cherry), in containers holding > 2 l (CN 2208 90 48)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Obstbranntwein (ausgenommen Pflaume, Birne, Kirsche), in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 l Inhalt (KN 2208 90 48)
new_classes.definition_en: Spirituous beverage obtained exclusively by alcoholic fermentation and distillation of fruit other than plums, pears or cherries (e.g. apricots, bilberries, raspberries, blackberries, currants, strawberries, apples), put up in containers holding more than 2 litres. Corresponds to CN subheading 2208 90 48.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the three main discriminating criteria for CN 2208 90 48: (1) the beverage is a fruit spirit obtained exclusively by alcoholic fermentation and distillation of fruit other than plums, pears or cherries (encoded via OtherFruitSpiritQuality and OtherFruitMashDistillationProcess); (2) it is presented in containers holding more than 2 litres (LargeContainer); (3) it is not a plum/pear/cherry spirit (complement of PlumPearCherryFruitSpiritQuality) and not a liqueur (complement of LiqueurSpiritousQuality). A small gap remains: the legal text specifies that the fruit spirit must be obtained 'exclusively' by fermentation and distillation (no blending with other spirits permitted). This exclusivity condition is captured semantically in the class definition and OtherFruitMashDistillationProcess, but cannot be fully formalised in OWL without a closed-world assumption. The specific enumeration of example fruits (apricots, bilberries, raspberries, etc.) is reflected in the definition text but cannot be exhaustively enumerated as OWL restrictions without a complete closed list, hence a minor score reduction.
